methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: HistoSonics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.25B

data.narrative:
Formation Bio is a Series D biotech company with a last post-money valuation of $1.7B and $372M raised to date. The valuation analysis employs a peer set of 5 private companies in the same sector and stage, supplemented by 3 public biotech comparables.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $1.7B last post-money valuation, implying $2.5B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector and adjacent-stage companies—Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Insitro, HistoSonics, and Colossal Biosciences—yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range from $1.5B to $5.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed; the Biotech & Health sector median P/S is 7.4x across three public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), but without a revenue figure, this method does not produce a meaningful valuation. Combining the two applicable methods produces a triangulated range of $2.0B to $2.9B, with a median of $2.5B. Key uncertainties include the typical variance in peer valuations, stage-specific advancement multiples in the biotech sector, and the absence of near-term revenue visibility for the target.
